business.headline: India-EU trade deal to be signed by end-2026, says Von der LeyenIn a bilateral meeting between Indian and EU delegations on the sidelines of the ongoing G7 Summit in France, the two sides also resolved to step up security and defence cooperation.

UK-India trade deal to take effect on July 15 despite dispute over steel tariffsUnder the deal, India will cut whisky tariffs to 40 per cent from 150 per cent, and automotives from 10% under a quota to 100 per cent, with Britain cutting tariffs on goods such as clothes, footwear and some food.

Dubai vs India: Which real estate market gives more bang for your buck?As property prices rise across Indian metros, investors are increasingly comparing Dubai with Mumbai, Delhi-NCR and Bengaluru. Larger homes, higher yields and tax-free returns are sharpening Dubai's appeal.

IT, PSU bank stocks lead gains as markets extend rally on falling oil pricesThe S&P BSE Sensex rose 347.14 points, or 0.45%, to settle at 77,155.62, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 96.55 points, or 0.40%, to close at 24,085.70.

IDBI Bank surges 19%: What's driving the sudden buying frenzy?IDBI Bank shares surged nearly 19% as renewed privatisation hopes and heavy trading lifted the stock. The rally reflects investor focus on the stalled stake sale, block deals and improving fundamentals.

SpaceX enters world's top 5 club: Elon Musk's company now worth more than AmazonSpaceX jumped past Amazon within days of its market debut after a sharp Wall Street rally. The surge, driven partly by options trading and index inclusion hopes, has intensified debate over its valuation.

Air India launches 'Basic' fare on domestic flights. What's included, what's notAir India has introduced a new Basic fare on select domestic routes that excludes complimentary meals. The pilot gives budget-conscious travellers a lower entry fare while leaving existing full-service fare options intact.

KPMG's AI-written report praising AI withdrawn after fake cases surfaceKPMG withdrew its global AI report after several organisations challenged fabricated case studies. The episode has sharpened concerns over AI hallucinations and the need for human verification.

A trade every 35 minutes? Trump's portfolio activity raises eyebrowsA financial disclosure showed US President Donald Trump's investment accounts made 3,642 trades in 90 days. The filing has prompted ethics questions, calls for an investigation and debate over conflicts of interest.

India isn't facing a 1991 crisis. But another challenge is taking shapeIndia has built a formidable forex cushion. But in an increasingly unpredictable world, will that be enough if foreign money starts drying up?

Stock market opens higher for fourth day as Brent crude slips below $79The S&P BSE Sensex was up 179.03 points, or 0.23%, at 76,987.51 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 46.45 points, or 0.19%, to trade at 24,035.60. The Nifty once again remained above the psychologically important 24,000 mark.

Jio may file $4 billion IPO papers before Reliance AGMReliance Jio may file draft papers for a $4 billion IPO within days, possibly before Reliance Industries' Friday AGM. The move would revive focus on one of India's most anticipated listings after delays and a slowdown in the IPO market.

Pizza Hut sold for $2.7 billion: Pizza chain many grew up with gets a new ownerYum Brands has agreed to sell Pizza Hut for $2.7 billion, splitting China from the rest of the business. The deal gives Yum China greater control in a key market and offers LongRange Capital a chance to revive the global chain.

Gold rallies, oil prices retreat on optimism over US-Iran peace dealGold rose on Tuesday after an interim US-Iran deal eased pressure on oil prices. Lower inflation fears also reduced expectations of a Federal Reserve rate hike later this year.

Why India's wealthy are quietly buying second residencies abroadThey are not leaving India. But they want the option to, just in case. From Dubai to Portugal to Greece, promoters and business families are spending crores on second residencies abroad. It is not about emigration. It is about insurance.

8th Pay Commission: Memorandum deadline ends. What have unions asked forThe deadline for 8th Pay Commission proposals has ended, bringing employee and pensioner demands before the panel. The next phase will test which calls on salary, DA, fitment and pensions shape the final recommendations.

Sensex ends 544 points higher, Nifty nears 24,000; HUL up 2%The S&P BSE Sensex climbed 544.15 points, or 0.71%, to settle at 76,808.48, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 135.25 points, or 0.57%, to close at 23,989.15, just shy of the crucial 24,000 mark.

11 digital tricks your bank can no longer play on youThe RBI has named and banned 11 specific digital manipulation tactics that banks use on their apps and websites to push products you never asked for. Here is what each one means and how it was being used against you.

US-Iran deal eases oil, but India's petrol relief may take longerThe US-Iran deal has reopened the Strait of Hormuz and cooled crude prices. But India may not see immediate fuel relief as supply chains and inventories recover.

World's 1st trillionaire says a quadrillion-dollar fortune 'not impossible'Elon Musk said becoming the world's first quadrillionaire is "not impossible" and linked it to factories on the Moon and Mars. He also said dollars may no longer matter in a future shaped by AI, robots and interplanetary industry.

entertainment.headline: The Grammys' Asian pop category and the politics of belonging The Recording Academy has announced a new Best Asian Pop Music Performance category at the Grammys. The move has revived questions about whether recognition also reinforces separation for Asian artists.

Spider-Man: Brand New Day advance bookings begin in India, but there's a catch Sony Pictures India has opened advance bookings for Spider-Man: Brand New Day. The India rollout adds multiple premium formats, with the movie releasing on July 30.

Internet 'disgusted' by Durex Pakistan's Obsession-inspired ad over consent concerns Durex Pakistan has drawn online criticism for an Instagram post themed around the horror film Obsession. Users said the ad ignored the film's disturbing consent context and made light of non-consensual sex.

Every decade, a new story: Tina Ahuja on parents Govinda-Ssunita's divorce rumours Tina Ahuja has said repeated rumours about parents Govinda and Ssunita Ahuja's marriage disturb her. Her remarks underline how years of public speculation have affected the family beyond the couple.

Orry makes Rs 76 lakh from one Insta Reel. Breakdown of his fame, income, story Orhan Awatramani aka Orry has explained how he earns through reels, brand deals and paid appearances. His remarks on charging up to Rs 25 lakh for private events and making Rs 76 lakh from one reel drew attention to the business of online fame.

Karan Johar bets on Alpha trailer, says Alia and Sharvari are 'Block a Buster' Karan Johar is rooting for Alpha. While praising Alia Bhatt and Sharvari's "girl power" action duo, his latest post also addressed the chatter sparked by his teaser reshare, where Sharvari wasn't tagged.

Bandhu 2.0: Fans praise Shahid, Kriti and Rashmika's chemistry in Cocktail 2 song Bandhu 2.0 brings Tum Hi Ho Bandhu back in Shahid Kapoor, Kriti Sanon and Rashmika Mandanna's Cocktail 2. The recreated song has been praised by fans as a tribute to the original version.

Remember Ishaan's brother Yohaan from Taare Zameen Par? He's a UK dentist now Sachet Seth, who played Yohaan in Taare Zameen Par, is now a registered dentist in the UK. His move from child actor to medical professional, along with his interest in flying, has drawn fresh attention.

Samantha meets CM Vijay, says he was meant for something bigger in a glowing note Samantha met Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay in Chennai and congratulated him on his election victory. In an Instagram note, she praised his move into public life and said his journey could inspire young people.

Pawan Kalyan meets fan batting rare genetic disorder, extends treatment support Pawan Kalyan met 17-year-old Niranjan at his Hanumakonda home after learning of his wish. He offered aid, prayers and support while saying the teenager inspired him.

Meet the full cast of Aamir Khan's Batwara 1947, led by Sunny Deol-Preity Zinta The character posters of Batwara 1947 featuring the principal cast have been unveiled. The reveal underlines the film's focus on courage and survival, set in the backdrop of the India-Pakistan partition.

India's Got Latent heads to Netflix, Samay Raina to shoot exclusive special episode Samay Raina's India's Got Latent is set to return next month on Netflix and YouTube. The move expands the show's reach and includes a separate Netflix-only special instalment.

Why Chidambaram's Balan: The Boy could be Malayalam cinema's surprise package Cannes acclaim, Ram Gopal Varma's bold prediction and the creative pairing of Manjummel Boys director Chidambaram and Jithu Madhavan have turned Balan: The Boy into one of Malayalam cinema's most intriguing underdogs.

House of the Dragon Season 3: Alliances, betrayals, wars and all you need to know House of the Dragon Season 3 streams in India on JioHotstar from June 22 as the Targaryen civil war erupts. Early reviews say it delivers the scale, betrayals and emotional pay-off fans were waiting for.

Sanchita Ugale death: Smita Bansal, Sarwam Kulkarni call for mental health support Television actor Sanchita Ugale's death has prompted emotional reactions from the industry. Actors Smita Bansal and Sarwam Kulkarni have spoken about the importance of mental health while urging people not to speculate as the investigation continues.

Alpha trailer review: Sita sets Lanka ablaze, makers spill full plot. What's left? The Alpha trailer lays out Alia Bhatt's assassin-origin story and a Hrithik Roshan crossover. It gives the YRF Spy Universe film a clearer identity, even as it leaves little mystery intact.

Alpha trailer X reviews: Hrithik Roshan's cameo hypes Alia-Sharvari spy film Hrithik Roshan's brief appearance in the Alpha trailer has sparked intense reactions on social media. The cameo has fuelled curiosity around the film even as reactions to the trailer remain divided.

Meet the OTT stars who quit promising careers to follow their acting passion Several popular OTT stars gave up stable careers, prestigious degrees and high-paying jobs to pursue acting. Read on to find out who left behind secure professions to become some of the biggest names on streaming platforms.

Why Main Vaapas Aaunga earned more on Tuesday than Friday at box office Main Vaapas Aaunga recorded a weekday box office jump, collecting Rs 1.90 crore on Tuesday. The rise has been linked to word of mouth, social media chatter and Imtiaz Ali's loyal audience.

general news.headline: Trump and Pezeshkian digitally sign US-Iran MoU to end conflict, reopen HormuzUS President Donald Trump and Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian digitally signed a memorandum of understanding to end the conflict and reopen the Strait of Hormuz. The deal includes sanctions relief, resumed Iranian oil exports, talks on implementation, and a 60-day framework for negotiating a broader agreement.

Safety of lakhs of Indian seafarers of high importance to us, says PM Modi to TrumpDays after three Indian sailors were killed in a US strike in the Gulf, PM Modi urged Donald Trump to ensure freedom of navigation and the safety of seafarers.

If PM Modi is leader and India is attacked, we'll help: Trump's big promiseSpeaking to reporters on the sidelines of the G7 Summit in France after his meeting with PM Narendra Modi, US President Donald Trump described their conversation as "very good". This was their first one-on-one meeting since Operation Sindoor in May 2025.

GHA 0-0 PAN Live: Panama keeping Semenyo quietGhana (GHA) vs Panama (PAN) Live, FIFA World Cup 2026: England have laid down an early marker in Group L. Now the spotlight shifts to Toronto, where Ghana and Panama begin their World Cup journeys knowing three points would be enough to keep pace with the early group leaders.

Age catches up? Cristiano Ronaldo blanks as Portugal held 1-1 by DR CongoFIFA World Cup 2026: Cristiano Ronaldo's record-breaking World Cup dream remains on hold after a frustrating night in Houston. Portugal may have escaped with a point against DR Congo, but questions over their creativity and dependence on a 41-year-old icon are only growing louder.

El Nino has arrived: Forecasts indicate it's not slowing till 2027Early climate forecasts show a powerful El Niño taking shape over the tropical Pacific. The projected warming could unsettle India's monsoon and intensify global weather extremes through 2027.

He gets you by surprise: Trump reveals what makes PM Modi 'tough'Donald Trump praised Narendra Modi as a 'tough trader' and a 'killer' as they held their first bilateral meeting in over a year on the sidelines of the G7 Summit in France.

Indian seafarer's mortal remains reach home eight days after US strikeShiv Anand Chaurasia's body reached his Deoria village eight days after he was killed in a US military strike. His family held up the funeral rites while seeking compensation, a job, schooling support and martyr status.

India has great friend in White House till I'm President: Trump to India TodayDonald Trump told India Today at the G7 Summit in France that India would have a great friend in the White House while he remained President. He paired that assurance with warm praise for PM Narendra Modi and support for India.

Live: Trump says US-Iran deal will be signed within next 48 hoursUS-Israel-Iran Live Updates: A proposed US-Iran agreement moved closer to reality after Tehran indicated that Presidents Donald Trump and Masoud Pezeshkian could personally sign the accord expected to be formalised in Switzerland on Friday. The development came as senior US officials disclosed details of the 14-point memorandum of understanding after days of secrecy surrounding the negotiations. In Tehran, Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmail Baghaei said a presidential signing ceremony remained "on the table", while officials in Washington stressed that the agreement was still preliminary and that either side could walk away before a final deal is reached. Speaking at the close of the G7 summit in France, Trump defended the accord, saying it had helped avert a wider economic crisis triggered by the conflict that erupted after US and Israeli strikes on Iran in February. He pointed to increased maritime traffic through the Strait of Hormuz and easing oil prices as signs of stabilisation, but warned that military action could resume if Tehran failed to honour its commitments. Under the framework, a 60-day negotiation period would begin after signing. The proposal includes limited sanctions relief, toll-free passage through the Strait of Hormuz and a commitment by Iran to dilute part of its highly enriched uranium stockpile. Follow indiatoday.in for the latest updates on US-Iran deal.

US drops 'Indo' from US military command name. What it means for IndiaThe Trump administration has restored the US Pacific Command name, reversing the 2018 move to call it US Indo-Pacific Command. Does the renaming reveal a change in strategy for the US in the Indian Ocean Region (IOR)? Is the US giving up its IOR plans after ditching its allies in the Middle East?

Trump says no one deliberately targeted Iran girls' school, probe continuesTrump said the February strike on a girls' school in Minab was not intentional and remains under investigation. The remarks came as Reuters reported US forces were likely responsible and officials examined outdated intelligence.

Bengal CM to revive colonial-era Gorkha force that 'Mamata tried to kill'. Here's whyWest Bengal CM Suvendu Adhikari announced the recruitment of more than 1,000 Gorkha youths into the Eastern Frontier Rifles, which Darjeeling MP Raju Bista said, was "Mamata Banerjee was trying to kill". So, what is the Eastern Frontier Rifles? Why does it have a special place in the Gorkha community?

Why Bangladesh is tense over construction of tallest Lord Ram statueConstruction of an 81-foot Lord Ram statue in Bangladesh has been paused after threats from radical Islamist groups. The move has triggered protests and renewed debate over religious freedom and minority rights under Tarique Rahman.

In 8th stage of genocide: Anti-India Ilhan Omar spews venom at Muslim-American eventUS Congresswoman and Democratic Party member Ilhan Omar, known for her anti-India stance, has accused the country of being in the eighth stage of genocide at an event organised by the Indian American Muslim Council. Her allegations stand in stark contrast to India's track record and the growth of minorities in the country.

How Shubman Gill battled cramps, Lucknow heat for heroic Afghanistan centuryIND vs AFG, 2nd ODI: Shubman Gill overcame cramps and the intense Lucknow heat to smash a brilliant 154 off 110 balls, leading India to a 170-run win over Afghanistan and an unassailable 2-0 series lead.

India's Got Latent heads to Netflix, Samay Raina to shoot exclusive special episodeSamay Raina's India's Got Latent is set to return next month on Netflix and YouTube. The move expands the show's reach and includes a separate Netflix-only special instalment.

When India removed barricades from US Embassy and why the 2013 memory is back in 2026In 2013, the arrest and strip-search of an Indian diplomat in New York prompted a fierce and coordinated diplomatic retaliation from New Delhi. More than a decade later, three Indian sailors are dead, killed by the US Navy, and India's response has been markedly more restrained.

Your reporters are much nicer than mine: Trump quips beside PM Modi at G7For nearly a decade, Donald Trump has frequently clashed with journalists, particularly during press conferences and television interviews. He has often accused mainstream media organisations of bias and has repeatedly labelled unfavourable coverage as fake news.

Students being butchered, suppressed: Rahul Gandhi's criticism of education systemAccording to the Congress, the 'Chhatron Ki Goonj' initiative aims to highlight challenges faced by students due to alleged paper leaks, rising examination costs, and what the party describes as the government's failure to ensure a fair and transparent education and recruitment system.

The reality of NEET re-exam leak claims: What we found in Kota is revealingHandcrafted ByOur Editors Students in Kota preparing for the June 21 NEET reexamination say they continue to encounter claims of paper leaks through Telegram, WhatsApp and phone calls. While the government has moved to restrict Telegram ahead of the exam, conversations on the ground suggest many students and teachers view the platform as only one part of a much larger ecosystem fuelled by rumours, anxiety and misinformation.

India Women hammer Netherlands, leapfrog Australia to go top of tableWomen's T20 World Cup, IND vs NED: India hammered the Netherlands by 95 runs in Leeds on Wednesday to climb to the top of Group A, overtaking Australia on net run rate.

Full text of 14-point draft agreement between US and IranA draft US-Iran memorandum sets out a ceasefire, Strait of Hormuz reopening and limited relief for Tehran. If approved, it would launch 60 days of negotiations towards a binding deal while uncertainty remains over the final text.

Internet 'disgusted' by Durex Pakistan's Obsession-inspired ad over consent concernsDurex Pakistan has drawn online criticism for an Instagram post themed around the horror film Obsession. Users said the ad ignored the film's disturbing consent context and made light of non-consensual sex.

sports.headline: 'Messi, Messi' chants rain down on Ronaldo as Portugal held by DR Congo FIFA World Cup 2026: Portugal opened their World Cup campaign with a 1-1 draw against DR Congo as Cristiano Ronaldo again failed to score. The result and the Messi chants around him sharpened the early contrast between football's two long-time rivals.

Thomas Tuchel gets it right as England overturn Croatia to win World Cup opener FIFA World Cup 2026: Thomas Tuchel's halftime changes proved decisive as England recovered from a sluggish first half to beat Croatia 4-2 in their World Cup opener, with Jude Bellingham leading the fightback.

GHA 0-0 PAN Live: Panama keeping Semenyo quiet Ghana (GHA) vs Panama (PAN) Live, FIFA World Cup 2026: England have laid down an early marker in Group L. Now the spotlight shifts to Toronto, where Ghana and Panama begin their World Cup journeys knowing three points would be enough to keep pace with the early group leaders.

Cristiano Ronaldo blanks as Portugal held 1-1 by DR Congo FIFA World Cup 2026: Cristiano Ronaldo's record-breaking World Cup dream remains on hold after a frustrating night in Houston. Portugal may have escaped with a point against DR Congo, but questions over their creativity and dependence on a 41-year-old icon are only growing louder.

How Shubman Gill battled cramps, Lucknow heat for heroic Afghanistan century IND vs AFG, 2nd ODI: Shubman Gill overcame cramps and the intense Lucknow heat to smash a brilliant 154 off 110 balls, leading India to a 170-run win over Afghanistan and an unassailable 2-0 series lead.

India Women hammer Netherlands, leapfrog Australia to go top of table Women's T20 World Cup, IND vs NED: India hammered the Netherlands by 95 runs in Leeds on Wednesday to climb to the top of Group A, overtaking Australia on net run rate.

In oppressive Lucknow heat, Shubman Gill leads India to his 1st series win India captain Shubman Gill won his first ODI series as a captain after India took an unassailable 2-0 lead against Afghanistan after the 2nd game of the series. Shubman led from the front with a terrific hundred in the oppressive heat in Lucknow.

Shreyanka Patil suffers freak foot injury, stretchered off from park against Netherlands India suffered a major injury scare during their Women's T20 World Cup clash against the Netherlands after Shreyanka Patil twisted her right ankle while fielding and had to be stretchered off the ground at Headingley.

World Cup Highlights: Ronaldo disappoints as Congo take a point in 1-1 draw Portugal (POR) vs DR Congo (COD) Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: (POR 1-1 COD). DR Congo have taken a point of the mighty Portugal here in Houston. Cristiano Ronaldo had a poor outing having been less involved in the game as it went deeper.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi pulls off stunning acrobatic catch vs Afghanistan A: Watch IND A vs AFG A: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi produced a stunning acrobatic catch and followed it up with a brisk 38-run knock as India A thrashed Afghanistan A by 101 runs in the Tri-Nation Series in Dambulla.

Vozinha now has Instagram followers 22 times his country's populationThe FIFA World Cup has the ability to change the life of any footballer in the world. Cape Verde goalkeeper Vozinha got to know that first hand after his Instagram following became 22 times of his country's population.

Dambulla tri-series: Tilak Varma and India A make amends, storm into the finalIndia A vs Afghanistan A: Tilak Varma struck 59 and led India A to a 101-run win over Afghanistan A in Dambulla. The result sealed a tri-series final spot after criticism followed the chaotic loss to Sri Lanka A.

Australia Women keep India on their toes, storm past Bangladesh with easeWomen's T20 World Cup, AUS vs BAN: Australia continued their impressive run in the tournament with a dominant nine-wicket win over Bangladesh, strengthening their position at the top of the group and piling pressure on India heading into the remaining matches.

After calls to drop him for discipline, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's bat fails to talkTalent tri-series: Sanjay Manjrekar said Vaibhav Sooryavanshi should have been dropped after the Sri Lanka A altercation. Retained against Afghanistan A, the teenager made 38 but again failed to convert his start on June 17 in Dambulla.

Serena Williams reveals daughter Olympia's role in Wimbledon comebackSerena Williams revealed that daughter Olympia inspired her Wimbledon 2026 comeback, prompting a reunion with sister Venus Williams in the women's doubles event after receiving a wildcard entry.

Twin tons: Ishan Kishan races into 2027 World Cup picture, Gill imperious as everShubman Gill and Ishan Kishan struck rapid hundreds as India tore into Afghanistan in Lucknow. Kishan's surge sharpened his World Cup case, while Gill underlined his command of the format.

Messi, Mbappe, Haaland all scored. Will Cristiano Ronaldo follow suit vs DR Congo?FIFA World Cup 2026: Messi, Mbappe and Haaland all shattered milestones on a remarkable World Cup night. Now Ronaldo steps in with a chance to make six-tournament scoring history for Portugal.

2nd ODI: Yashasvi Jaiswal fails in rare chance, Prince Yadav makes debutIndia reshuffled their XI for the second ODI in Lucknow, handing Prince Yadav a debut and Yashasvi Jaiswal an opening role. Nitish Kumar Reddy's injury scare forced the late changes.

Austria score 3 past Jordan to go second behind Argentina in Group JFIFA World Cup 2026: Austria beat Jordan 3-1 in Santa Clara after Yazan Al-Arab's own goal shattered a 1-1 deadlock. Jordan's spirited World Cup debut still forced Austria to rely on a stoppage-time penalty to close it out.

Rafa on Netflix, magic on the pitch: Lionel Messi's new World Cup routineLionel Messi said he has been watching Rafael Nadal's documentary on Netflix as he prepared for Argentina's FIFA World Cup opener against Algeria. The 38-year-old scored a hat-trick on Wednesday, equalling Miroslav Klose's all-time record for most goals in the competition.

Should Lionel Messi have seen red vs Algeria? Fans debate decisionFIFA World Cup 2026: Lionel Messi hit a hat-trick as Argentina beat Algeria 3-0 in their World Cup opener. A disputed challenge on Aissa Mandi, ignored by the referee and VAR, kept the noise alive.

After hat-trick heroics, Messi reveals why he cried on record World Cup nightLionel Messi said he had gone through "difficult days" away from football before his record-equalling hat-trick against Algeria, explaining the emotional scenes that followed his opening goal in Argentina's World Cup opener.

Highlights: India qualify for tri-series final after huge win vs AFG-AIndia (IND-A) vs Afghanistan (AFG-A) Highlights, Tri-Series 2026: AFG (218-all-out in 36.5 ov) chasing 320 runs. India A have bagged a massive victory against Afghanistan - by 101 runs. Nishant Sindhu and Yash Thakur bowl out Afghanistan A and have sent India A to the final of the tournament.

Magical Messi sinks Algeria with hat-trick, equals all-time goal-scoring recordLionel Messi scored a historic hat trick as Argentina opened their FIFA World Cup title defence with a commanding 3-0 victory over Algeria in Kansas City on Tuesday, drawing level with Miroslav Klose as the tournament's all-time leading goalscorer.

Hungry like a debutant, Rohit Sharma targets big statement in Lucknow ODIIndia lead Afghanistan 1-0. The 2nd ODI at Lucknow's Ekana Stadium today is a chance to seal the series, but one subplot will shadow everything else from the first ball.

From Al Qaeda killing his father to scoring at World Cup, meet Iraq's captain fantasticFIFA World Cup 2026: Aymen Hussein scored Iraq's lone goal in a 4-1 World Cup defeat to Norway after a journey marked by war and personal loss. His equaliser, days after a seven-hour detention in Chicago, underlined why he stands as a national symbol of resilience in Iraq.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
